scope: Continue implementing feats from updated descriptions using existing engines and safe metadata.
source_repo: foundryvtt-swse-main (22).zip with phases 1-13 changed-file patches overlaid before this pass

changes: Attack options can now require Aim or Charge context instead of only warning about it.
Attack options can now require weapon text, damage type, excluded damage type, and target type.
This allows feats such as Droid Hunter, Sport Hunter, Riflemaster, Disabler, Anointed Hunter, and Mandalorian Training to be represented without fake static bonuses.
Attack reroll options can now be filtered by attack type, vehicle weapon status, weapon text, and damage type.
Gunnery Specialist can now expose only for Vehicle Weapon attacks instead of every attack.
Attack reroll options are now built after hit/miss resolution so missed-attack triggers such as Desperate Gambit can be filtered correctly.
Reroll context now carries the original attack options, target, and isHit state.

high_confidence_executable_changes: Forceful Grip and Forceful Slam now expose +2 Use the Force bonuses only for matching Force Power activation contexts.
Keen Force Mind now exposes +2 Use the Force only for mind-affecting Force Power contexts.
Gunnery Specialist now exposes a once-per-encounter Vehicle Weapon attack reroll that keeps the second result.
Instinctive Attack now exposes a Force Point attack reroll that keeps the better result.
Galactic Alliance Military Training, Ion Shielding, Shake It Off, and Sadistic Strike now use resourceRules/conditionTrack rules already read by existing resolver/listener code.
Droid Hunter can now apply damage bonuses through roll context when the target is a droid and when ion damage is identified.
Sport Hunter, Riflemaster, and Disabler now expose weapon-family damage-die/property metadata instead of manual placeholders.
Attack reroll filtering now has hit/miss context available.
metadata_only_or_future_hook_changes: Rancor Crush, Bone Crusher, Frightening Presence, Quick Comeback, Forceful Strike, Forceful Telekinesis, Jedi Heritage, Jedi Familiarity, Spacer's Surge, Binary Mind, Poison Resistance, Tumble Defense, and several action/reaction feats now have explicit metadata but still need their consuming UI/event hooks before they are fully automatic.
Armor/Exotic proficiency metadata is now explicit, but equipment proficiency enforcement still depends on the equipment/attack preparation path consuming proficiencyGrants.
Two-weapon and Dual Weapon Mastery metadata is explicit, but full multiattack UI needs to consume multiAttackRules.

validation.jsonlPackParse: packs/feats.db parsed as valid JSONL with 419 rows
